@@ -2763,3 +2763,3 @@ - * The inverse hyperbolic sine of x is defined to be a function such that - * asinh({@linkplain Math#sinh sinh(x)}) = x for any x. - * Note that both range and domain of the exact asinh are unrestricted. + * The inverse hyperbolic sine of x is defined to be the function such that + * asinh({@linkplain Math#sinh sinh(x)}) = x for any x. + * Note that both domain and range of the exact asinh are unrestricted. @@ -2773,1 +2773,1 @@ - * negative infinity with the same sign as the argument. + * infinity with the same sign as the argument. @@ -2777,1 +2777,0 @@ - *

The computed result must be within 2.5 ulps of the exact result. @@ -2780,0 +2779,1 @@ + *

@@ -2176,3 +2176,3 @@ - * The inverse hyperbolic sine of x is defined to be a function such that - * asinh({@linkplain Math#sinh sinh(x)}) = x for any x. - * Note that both range and domain of the exact asinh are unrestricted. + * The inverse hyperbolic sine of x is defined to be the function such that + * asinh({@linkplain Math#sinh sinh(x)}) = x for any x. + * Note that both domain and range of the exact asinh are unrestricted. @@ -2186,1 +2186,1 @@ - * negative infinity with the same sign as the argument. + * infinity with the same sign as the argument.

@@ -2763,1 +2763,1 @@
-    * Method :
+    *  Method :
@@ -2765,7 +2765,7 @@
-    *  Based on
-    *    asinh(x) = sign(x) * log [ |x| + sqrt(x*x+1) ]
-    *   we have
-    *   asinh(x) := x  if  1+x*x=1,
-    *            := sign(x)*(log(x)+ln2)) for large |x|, else
-    *            := sign(x)*log(2|x|+1/(|x|+sqrt(x*x+1))) if|x|>2, else
-    *            := sign(x)*log1p(|x| + x^2/(1 + sqrt(1+x^2)))
+    *   Based on
+    *     asinh(x) = sign(x) * log [ |x| + sqrt(x*x+1) ]
+    *    we have
+    *    asinh(x) := x  if  1+x*x=1,
+    *             := sign(x)*(log(x)+ln2)) for large |x|, else
+    *             := sign(x)*log(2|x|+1/(|x|+sqrt(x*x+1))) if|x|>2, else
+    *             := sign(x)*log1p(|x| + x^2/(1 + sqrt(1+x^2)))
